rulings.Case Title: PATRICK KELLY VS. THOMAS WHITE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Patrick Kelly'S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Interrogatories And Document Request'S And Request'S For Admissions And Monetary Sanction Request Defendant And/Or Its Attorney Of Record
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, November 20, 2012, Line 25, Plaintiff Patrick Kelly's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Interrogatories And Document Request's And Requests For Admissions And Monetary Sanction Request Defendant And/Or Its Attorney Of Record. The court adopts the recommendation of Pro Tem Judge Watson as follows: Denied. The discovery requests will not lead to the discovery of admissible evidence. Further, Mr. White cannot be required to furnish additional information about his personal activities without potentially trenching upon his Fifth Amendment protection. Monetary sanctions are denied. The court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: INFAC MANAGEMENT CORPORATION, VS. COMDISCO INVESTMENT GROUP, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Joint Ntc Of Motion To Strike Portions Of Pltfs Second Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, November 20, 2012, Line 26, DEFENDANT VERNON LOUCKS' Joint Notice Of Motion To Strike Portions Of Plaintiff's Second Amended Complaint. The motion to strike is granted without leave to amend as to paragraph 26 of the 2nd AC. The allegation that defendant Vernon Loucks released his ability to execute on his loans contradicts the carve-out language of the release, which is attached to the complaint. The motion is denied as to paragraph 27. Paragraph 2.6 is reasonably susceptible to plaintiff's interpretation that Vernon Loucks cannot act as Comdisco's agent and the Court cannot resolve that issue on this motion to strike. See Aragon-Haas v. Family Security Ins. Services, Inc., (1991) 231 Cal.App.3d 232, 239 ("While plaintiffs' interpretation of the contract may prove invalid, it [is] improper to resolve the issue against her solely on her own pleading.") The Court strikes without leave to amend only that portion of paragraph 28 that refers to the settlement agreement. The Court denies defendant's request to strike the prayer for permanent injunctive relief. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: RICHARD SINCLAIR ESQ VS. LINDA CATRON et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Linda Catron'S Motion For Terminating Sanctions For Failure To Comply With Prior Court Orders
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, November 20, 2012, Line 5, Defendant Linda Catron's Motion For Terminating Sanctions For Failure To Comply With Prior Court Orders. The court adopts the recommendation of Pro Tem Judge Halperin as follows: Denied in part. The Court issues an order to show cause set for hearing on January 11, 2012 why terminating sanctions should not be imposed if Mr. Sinclair has not complied with all outstanding discovery orders by providing code-compliant statements of inability to comply pursuant to C.C.P. Section 2031.230 that explain the basis of his assertion of an inability to comply and the fate of the document requested or identified that existed but which he does not have. Any opposition brief to the OSC shall be filed and served by Mr. Sinclair no later than January 2, 2012 at 4:00 pm, and any reply brief shall be filed and served by January 7, 2012 at 4:00 pm. The court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: RICHARD SINCLAIR ESQ VS. LINDA CATRON et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Richard Sinclair'S Notice Of Motion And Plaintiff/Cross-Defendant Richard C. Sinclair, Esquire'S Motion For Ccp 128.7 And Ccp 2023.101 Sanctions And Attorney Fees Against Paul Frassetto, Esquire
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, November 20, 2012, Line 5, Plaintiff Richard Sinclair's Motion And Plaintiff/Cross-Defendant Richard C. Sinclair, Esq.'s Motion For CCP 128.7 And CCP 2023.101 Sanctions And Attorney Fees Against Paul Frassetto, Esq. The court adopts the recommendation of Pro Tem Judge Halperin as follows: Denied. Although titled as a motion for sanctions, this motion is an untimely motion for reconsideration of the orders. (See C.C.P. § 1008(a).) No new or different facts, circumstances, or law exist for reconsideration of any of the orders. The court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. A ny party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: GREGORY WOOD VS. OLENA BARANOVA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To Conduct Neuropsychological Examination, Pursuant To Ccp 2032.310
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, November 20, 2012, at 9:00 am, line 4, Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To Conduct Neuropsychological Examination, Pursuant To CCP 2032.310 Pro Tem Judge James Watson, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Counsel to appear. Each party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing. =302/JPT

rulings.Case Title: TRANSITION, LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED LIABILITY VS. WB COYLE A/K/A W.B. COYLE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Deposition Subpoena For Production Of Business Records;
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, November 20, 2012, at 9:00 am, line 2, DEFENDANTS WB COYLE A/K/A W.B. COYLE, TELEGRAPH HILL PROPERTIES INC., DANILO DESIGN GROUP, L.P.'S, Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Deposition Subpoena For Production Of Business Records Pro Tem Judge James Watson, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Deny the motion. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to jamesw@skwsf.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/JPT

If the tentative ruling is not contested, the court will sign the proposed order submitted by the moving party without need for any appearances. = (302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: TREMOLADA & COMPANY INC., VS. CHEMOIL CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, November 20, 2012, Line 21, DEFENDANT CHEMOIL CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Denied. Defendant failed to shift the burden. Under the clear and unambiguous terms of the 2011 Agreement, Plaintiff was entitled to a commission. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/EHG)
